titleStmt.title: SII 10.177: original edition by J. Ramayya Pantulu
No. 177. (A.R. No. 49 of 1909.) ON A PILLAR IN FRONT OF THE TEMPLE OF RĀMALINGĒŚVARA, SIRIPURAM, SATTENEPALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT. Ś. 1093.

summary.p: The first part states that Rājēndra-Chōḍa, son of Velanāṭi Kulōttuṅga Chōḍa-Gōṅka, who was lord of the sixteen thousand Āndhra country and who seized the town Kolanu and killed its chief Bhīma, made gifts of lands to the temple of Rāmēśvara of Siripuram one of the twelve pūṇḍis in Koṇḍapaḍumaṭi district and also to dancing girls attached to it. The second part says that a certain Bolli-Nāyaka gave 55 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the said temple. The third part refers to gifts made by other persons to the same temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti calamarttigaṇḍaḥ paranṛpa
jīvāpaharaṇakarayamadaṁḍaḥ
sadguṇaratnakaraṁḍaḥ kavijanaśi
ṣṭēṣṭavanajavanamārttaṇḍaḥ . yēna kṣitī
śvaravarēṇa manīṣivārtthiḥ puṇyāni ra
tnakalitātulajīvanāni saśvadvivriddhima
hitāni ciraṁttarāni dhattē samujvalaye
śōlalitōttamāni . Esya sphuranniśitabhīṣa
ṇakhaḍgadhārasaṁpūjitā vijayakanyaka
yā vibhāti nirbbhinnavairigajamastakapīṭha
jātamuktākṣatōjvalakarāṁbujayāruṇā
gra . sōyaṁ śrīvelanāṁṭigoṁkkanṛpati trai
lōkyadīpāṁkkura ttīvrājipraṇadāritārinika
ra ssaujanyaratnākaraḥ . prōdyallāṭa varāṭakuṁ
ttalapatī nirjjitya bāhāsinā tatsarvvasva
madājjagannutayaśaḥ kāmī nijasvāminē .
sakalōrvvījana rakṣaṇakṣamu daśāśāpū
rnnakīrttīlatāprakaṭan bhaṁḍanabhīmu
durjyayakulaprākāru satyapratu nsuka
vīṁdrāmarabhūjamun baḍigulārjjunu vī
rarājēṁdracōḍikumāruṁ braṇutiṁparēśṛśu
bhaṭakaṁṭīru nnudāruṁ brabhun . 1 Ila vela
nāṁṭigoṁkkavasudhēśutanūbhavuṁ ḍainacōḍa
bhūtalapati sōḍapaṭṭamu daṁbunaṁ gaṭṭi
bhujābalaṁbunaṁ ggolanu jayiṁcci yaṁddu ri
pukuṁjaru bhīmaniṁ jaṁpi Aṁdhrabhūtala
mu vadāṟuvēlu satatasthiti nēle surēṁdru
līlalan . 2 svasti samastapraśastisahitaṁ
śrīmanmahāmaṇḍalēśvara kulōttuṁggacōḍago
ṁkkayamahārājula priyātmajuṁḍaina rājē
ṁdracōḍikumāruṁḍu goṇḍapaḍumaṭaṁ
baṁḍreṇḍu vūṁḍla siripuṁbuna rāmēśva
radēvaraku dīpanivēdyabhōgaṁbuna kayyūriĀgnē
yaṁbuna dakṣiṇamukhaṁ baina daṇḍagaṭṭa ya
nu ceṟuvunu guḍi yuttaraṁbuna tāḍitōṁ
ṭayu nūri tūrppudiśābhāgaṁbunaṁdu
nūṟumaṭlu bhūmiyu śakavarṣaṁbulu
1087 nēṁṭi yuttarāyaṇa nimittaṁbuna
sthānapatiki sānulaku mānulaku naṭṭamaṁḍā
gonipinlakāṟaku sarvvakāraparihāra
ṁbugā niccina datti dēvaraku nivēdyabhūmi
kha5 bhīmīśvaradēvara khā nā0 . gauḍīśva
radēvara khā viṣṇudēvara kha2 gaṇēśvaru
ni kha 2 prōlērusāni khā sthānapatime
ḍajiyya kha3 mupparisāni nāca
sāninaga kha4 krommasāni māra kha3
mārāsānipaḍla kha3 kāṭāsāniva
lya kakha3 Āytasānibayya kha3 Ā
tyasānikāṭaka kha3 naḍvāsānikēta
kha3 mailāsānimāca kha3 prōlāsā
nicāma kha3 cevvāsānipārvvati kha
3 pērāsāniEḍapāpa kha3 muttāsā
nidāma kha3 naṭṭavulavāmamāreni kha6
mōkarisūre kha4 maddaliyavaṟna
kha4 maddaliyadēvana kha4 vāsikā
ḍa kha3 mēlunāyaka māra
marāju kha3 saṁklunayti kha2 dō
raprōle khānā0 goḍavunale khā na
10 virikamrēka kha2 ghaṁṭṭaEṟna kha
1 mrugguveṭṭiḍi māsakamma khā
kāsya veprōlela kha3 dēvara vi
Eṟayapākhya kha3 vāsanaca
lla kha3 dēvikavigomme kha3 Ila vela
nāṁṭṭicōḍinṛpu niccina dattamu dī
ni kanyathā dalaṁcina durvvinītuṁḍu ma
daṁ bbaṟi durddaśaṁ boṁddi vaṁddi yākulu
ṁḍai kālu prōlu soraṁ goṭṭina viḍmaṟa gā
na kaṁdda tā nelakoni rauravāṁbudhi mu
niṁggi yaḍaṁggu nanēka kālamulu . 3 ce
ṟuvulu nūṟu vannasa laciṁtyamu satra
mu lagrahāramulu guṟukoni lakṣagaṁ
ggulokokōṭiśivālayamulu sahasra
kaṁ baṟuvadivēlu vāvulu satādhvara
mulu vadivēlu brahmamuṁ jēṟicina A
ṁtta vātakamu siddhamu dīniki hiṁsa sē
sinan . 4 tana datta manyadattaṁ banaṁ jana
du svadatta me ṭlaṭla rakṣiṁpaniyataṁ ḍa
ṟuvadi vēlēṁ ḍlanavarata mamēdhyakīṭa
mai janiyiṁccun . 5 harahāsēṁddu
tuṣārasā pā radasitābhrairāvatōccai
śravāṁbaradugdhābdhisurāpagāṁburajata
prakhyātakīrttiprabhaṁ gara moppanusahakāra
vallabhunripaughaśrēṣṭu goṁkkakṣitīśvarasūnunu
velanāṁṭi cōḍi janapunu sāmānyamē pē rkkonanu . 6
śrīmanmahāmaṁḍalēśvara rājēṁdra
cōḍayarā
rāmanidhivyōmarājavatsaramulu varttillaṁgā kharava
tsaramunaṁ baragina [Aṭṭi] yīgiripaścimaṁ bulōṁbūṁ
ḍlu vaṁḍreṁ ḍapūrvvabhāti 7 . śrīkaraṁ bai yoppu siripuraṁ
buna nuttarāyaṇaṁbunayaṁdu ramaṇatōḍa
vaṟalaṁgga neṟiyana varatanūjuṁ ḍagu bolla
na bālēṁdubhūṣaṇunakuṁ baramabhaktiṁ dāṁḍi pāla
gōtruṇḍu manōjanibhuṁḍu gaḍu mahō
tsavamugi . maṇḍitaṁbugā nakhaṇḍadīpa
mu veṭṭi vārijāptacaṁdratārakamuga . 8
śakavarṣaṁbulu 1093 Agunēṁṭi Uttarā
yana saṁkrāṁttinimittamuna siripuramuśrī
mūlasthānamahādēvaraku bollināyakuṁḍu
tama taṁḍri yeṟṟamanāyakunaku guṁ ḍāsā niki dharmmā
rtthamugā nakhaṇḍadīpamunakuṁ beṭṭina goṟiyalu
55 vīniṁ jēkoni mrāniyabōyinikoḍku Ama
rabōyuṁḍu dana putrānupautrikamu nitya
māneṁḍu nēyi vōyaṁggalavāru .
siripuramusāsanilu
kaṭṭiraḍḍi kaṭṭiraḍḍi vīri śrī
rāmīśvaramahādēvaraku niccina tāḍitōṁṭa naḍa
siripuramubramminā
yakuṁḍu gauvirīsvaradē
varaku
dēvara brahmapurulu jakkana
bhaṭla khā sōmanabhaṭla khā ko
gautamagōtraṁtā saraya lavatti
nnayaku khā0 cannamallu
rāmanidhivyōmarāja vatsaramulu vartti
llaṁgā kharavatsaramuna na AṭṭiĪgiri
paścimaṁbulōṁba ṭṭaṁparvvabhāti
śrīkaraṁ bai yoppu siripuraṁbuna nuttarā yaṇaṁbuna
yaṁdu ramaṇatōḍa navaratanūjuṁḍa
brammanabālēṁdu nakuṁbaramabha
ktiṁdāṁḍipā janibhuṁ
ḍu gaḍumapē gānakhaṁḍadī
pamu veṭṭe vārijā gāśakavarṣaṁ
bulu 1093 saṁkrāṁttinimi
ttamuna siripu rāmīsvara
śrīmahādēvaraku nāyakuṁḍu tama taṁḍri
brammināya kommasāniki kēta
sānikani dharmmārtthamu ḍamunaku nicci
na guḍi tūrpputāḍitōṁṭa
rāmanidhivyō
varttillaṁgā
Ī giripa ḍluvaṁḍre
paramabhakti
veṭṭe vārijā
varṣaṁbulu
saṁkrāṁtti
yaṁḍu tama
dama talli
nakhaṁḍa
ḍitōṁṭa
śiripuri
nyaṁvōṁgā
dakṣiṇaṁ
kaṁbhaṁdakṣiṇa
paḍmaraikuṁṭa kaṁbhaṁdakṣi
ṇapaścima galatū
rpunaṁ kūḍale Āja
male
tikūḍa Uttarapaści
maUttara

Note.—The rest of the inscription is greatly damaged. This inscription really consists of three inscriptions—lines 1 to 86 constituting one inscription, lines 87 to 129, another inscription and the rest yet another inscription. There is no information available as to whether all these lines are inscribed on the same side or on different sides of the pillar.
Digital edition of SII 10.177 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
